ai_summary: Over the trailing ~12 months the dominant pattern is accumulation in tech and defense-adjacent industrials. Microsoft (MSFT) is the clear leader — ~79 disclosed purchases across 21 members — followed by chip exposure via TSM (~39) and AMD (~26). Defense/industrials show up through TransDigm (TDG), BWX Technologies (BWXT, nuclear), Hubbell (HUBB) and Corning (GLW). On the sell side, the notable moves are semiconductor trimming — Sen. Whitehouse's NVDA sale (up to $250K, Jun 2026) and Rep. Khanna unloading ASML, Micron (MU) and Ultra Clean (UCTT). Net: buying AI/compute and defense, lightening richly-valued chip names. Amounts are disclosed ranges and filings lag up to 45 days, so treat this as a lagging signal, not real-time. Not investment advice.
